data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Amazon minimizes choice overload by streamlining user decisions.
data.technical_spec: Predictive search, user history integration, and personalized shopping feed.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so Amazon leverages common design patterns.
data.technical_spec: Consistent global navigation and standardized checkout process.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Interactive elements on Amazon are sized appropriately for easy access.
data.technical_spec: Prominent call-to-action buttons and intuitive product filtering options.

data.title: Principle of Feedback
data.description: Amazon provides clear feedback on user actions to enhance user experience.
data.technical_spec: Cart notifications, delivery status updates, and easy return processes.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Amazon fuses aesthetics with functionality to create a pleasant user experience.
data.technical_spec: Clean interface design, consistent branding, and responsive layouts.
